behaviors.description: Trim conversation history to the most recent 10 messages when full history exceeds context window.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because this is deterministic trimming logic on input data shape/order. Downgrade check: lower than Unit is not applicable. Upgrade check: Integration/System/Agentic rejected because behavior text only requires 'trimmed to the most recent 10 messages' and does not require real DB/network/subprocess or model-quality judgment. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels are disqualified by the behavior being local transformation before LLM call, not cross-module failure evidence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the history-trimming function with >10 messages and assert output contains exactly the last 10 in original order; also assert <=10 inputs are unchanged.

behaviors.description: Generated response addresses the customer’s question (not generic/off-topic).
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because correctness depends on real LLM output relevance/quality and can vary run-to-run. Downgrade check: Integration with stubs would miss true relevance failures (MOCK-HIDE). Upgrade check: Workflow rejected because behavior describes a single generation quality criterion, not multiple agentic steps in sequence. REJECTION-GATE: 'addresses the customer's question rather than ... off-topic' is explicit model-output quality text, requiring real model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run a curated set of real customer prompts against the production model path and score relevance with a fixed rubric; fail if responses are generic/off-topic beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check identifies overly formal response for casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because 'correctly identifies' tone appropriateness is model-judgment quality, not just routing. Downgrade check: pure Integration would only verify trigger wiring, not correctness of tone detection. Upgrade check: Workflow considered but rejected; behavior requires one agentic judgment plus trigger, not validating the full multi-step agent pipeline outcome. REJECTION-GATE: behavior text focuses on identification accuracy for tone, which disqualifies Unit/Integration-only tests.
behaviors.test_description: Provide known too-formal candidate responses for casual tier to the real tone-check path and assert they are flagged and regeneration is invoked.

behaviors.description: Persist full exchange (user message, generated response, regenerations) with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because failure boundary is service-to-database persistence and record assembly across modules. Downgrade check: Unit is too low; a single function test would miss schema/transaction/wiring mistakes. Upgrade check: System rejected because behavior text does not require real networked DB/OS primitives; local test DB is sufficient. Agentic/Workflow rejected because this behavior is storage correctness, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute chatbot flow with deterministic stubbed LLM outputs including one regeneration, then query a local test DB to verify all exchange entries and timestamp fields/order are persisted correctly.

behaviors.description: If tone check triggers regeneration, second response replaces first so rejected draft is never shown.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is cross-module handoff/output-selection behavior (generation pipeline to customer-facing response). Downgrade check: Unit may miss real failure where modules pass wrong field/state. Upgrade check: Agentic rejected since condition is explicitly 'When the tone check triggers' (trigger assumed), so correctness here is replacement logic, not model quality. System rejected: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text. Workflow rejected: not a full multi-agent pipeline validation.
behaviors.test_description: Stub tone-check to force regeneration and provide distinct first/second drafts; run end-to-end service path in-process and assert delivered response equals second draft while first appears only in internal audit data.
